story: After I took my own life, there was no thunder, just forms. The rulekeepers said I still owed Harold the care I’d promised until his real death; to resolve it I had to prove he drove me to it, show a divorce underway, or find him a new wife, or else wait. I went to check on him. He was in his recliner, TV glowing. A figure slid through me, cold wire arm raised, and fired. I jolted back to my side. A breath later, Harold was beside me in that elsewhere.
implausible_knowledge: The rulekeepers said I still owed Harold the care I’d promised until his real death; to resolve it I had to prove he drove me to it, show a divorce underway, or find him a new wife, or else wait.
question: When Harold suddenly finds himself beside the narrator in the elsewhere right after being shot, what is he most likely to do next?
answer_with_knowledge: Tell her she no longer needs to wait.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask her what just happened to him.
